summary: Verified the new Sample Room Editor feature end-to-end. Backend pytest suite (test_visualizer_room_editor.py + test_visualizer_admin_rooms.py + test_visualizer_v3_polish.py) ran 22 passed + 1 skipped — matches the stated baseline. Frontend Playwright test exercised the full edit + create + delete round-trip on /admin/visualizer: 'Add room' button is present in the Sample Rooms card header, all 10 rooms have Edit buttons (data-testid 'visualizer-admin-room-edit-<id>'), the editor modal opens correctly with 4 SVG polygon handles (handles 0–3), URL-paste swaps the image, the floor/wall surface toggle correctly switches the polygon stroke colour (yellow #facc15 → cyan #38bdf8), 'Reset polygon to default' fires its toast, Cancel closes the modal without persisting, and a fresh-create flow with a pasted Unsplash URL successfully POSTed to /api/admin/visualizer/sample-rooms (200, returned new UUID 77ca697e-…) and the test room appeared in the grid + was cleaned up via DELETE (200). One pre-existing artifact spotted in the grid: 'Utility Room Floor' renders with the new 'Image unavailable (upstream 404)' fallback placeholder — exactly the symptom this editor was designed to let admins fix in-place, so it's actually a good demo of the feature working as intended.
critical_code_review_comments: SampleRoomEditor.jsx (~330 lines) is well-scoped — single concern, no duplicate state machinery, idempotent polygon scaling (naturalWidth/displayWidth), no _id leakage in payload (saves the API-shape directly).

Polygon dragging uses pointermove/pointerup on window with proper cleanup in useEffect — no listener leaks. setPointerCapture is called optionally so it degrades gracefully on browsers that don't support it.
Save button is correctly disabled when image_url is empty (verified: True before paste, False after) — prevents accidental empty submissions.

Frontend uploads are guarded client-side at 12 MB before hitting the wire, mirroring the backend 413 cap; the upload-image endpoint additionally enforces empty=422 / non-image=415, so the cost-money fal.ai call is only fired for legitimate images.
axios POST to /api/admin/visualizer/sample-rooms uses tokenHdr() (reads localStorage 'token' or 'access_token') — consistent with the rest of the admin app.
VisualizerAdmin.jsx mounts the modal conditionally and passes onSaved to refresh the rooms list — clean parent/child contract.

Minor (not blocking): the modal's overlay click-to-close swallows clicks anywhere outside the inner card; admins might lose unsaved polygon edits accidentally. Consider an 'unsaved changes' confirm if form is dirty. Not a regression vs current design.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 throwaway room 'PYTEST UI Throwaway' via the editor's Create flow, then deleted via API — clean.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sample Room Editor (/admin/visualizer 'Add room' + per-row 'Edit') is fully functional. Backend endpoints POST /api/admin/visualizer/upload-image (admin-only, validated for empty/oversize/non-image) and the existing POST /api/admin/visualizer/sample-rooms (now also handles upsert with id) are both green. The only remaining curiosity is that one seeded Unsplash URL (Utility Room Floor) is currently 404ing upstream — but that's exactly the case the editor lets admins fix without a Railway shell, so the feature is working as designed. No further regression testing needed unless backend visualizer routes change.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found this iteration.
